slides.title: What is a Parametric Curve?
slides.contentBeforeEquation: A parametric curve is expressed as
slides.contentEnd: in 3D. This describes motion or geometry along a path.

slides.title: Worked Example: Parametric Curve
slides.contentAfterEquation: for t from 0 to 1. It traces from (0,1) to (1,e).

slides.title: Curve Orientation
slides.contentBeforeEquation: Orientation determines direction along the curve. For
slides.contentAfterEquation: the forward direction is from (0,1) to (1,e). The opposite direction uses

slides.title: Derivatives of Parametric Curves
slides.contentBeforeEquation: The derivative gives the tangent vector direction and speed. For

slides.title: Worked Example: Tangent Vector
slides.contentEnd: . At t=1, tangent vector: (2, cos(1), e).

slides.title: Recap
slides.content: Key skills: 1) Identify curves. 2) Understand orientation. 3) Differentiate. 4) Draw tangent lines. 5) Recognize standard forms.
